content.text:
Using the previous analysis, propose a clean domain model.

Requirements:
- Claims contain multiple line items
- Coverage limits are tracked annually
- Partial approvals are possible
- Members can dispute decisions

Provide:
- Entities
- Value objects
- State machines
- Invariants

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness ŌĆö fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly ŌĆö if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highŌåÆmax: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only ŌĆö it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK ŌĆö model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER ŌĆö read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" ŌĆö whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) ŌĆö never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named ŌĆö don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Good. Here is a clean domain model grounded in insurance claims semantics:

---

## Domain Model: Insurance Claims Processing

---

### Entities

**`Member`**
The insured person. Holds a `PolicyId` and is the subject of coverage limits.
- `memberId`, `policyId`, `name`, `coverageYear`

---

**`Policy`**
The contract defining what is covered. Does not change during claim processing.
- `policyId`, `planType`, `effectiveDate`, `expirationDate`
- Has a collection of `CoverageRule` (maps `ServiceCategory` ŌåÆ `CoverageLimit`)

---

**`Claim`** *(aggregate root)*
The central aggregate. A member submits a claim for services rendered. Contains one or more `LineItem`s.
- `claimId`, `memberId`, `policyId`, `submittedAt`
- `lineItems: LineItem[]`
- `status: ClaimStatus` *(see state machine)*
- `adjudicationResult: AdjudicationResult | null`

Invariants:
- Must have at least one `LineItem`
- All `LineItem`s must reference a `ServiceDate` within the policy's effective period
- `adjudicationResult` may only be set when status is `UNDER_REVIEW`
- Once `CLOSED`, no mutations are allowed

---

**`LineItem`** *(child entity, not independently addressable)*
A single service billed within a claim.
- `lineItemId`, `serviceDate: ServiceDate`, `serviceCategory: ServiceCategory`
- `billedAmount: Money`, `approvedAmount: Money | null`
- `adjudicationNote: string | null`

Invariants:
- `approvedAmount` Ōēż `billedAmount`
- `approvedAmount` may be null until adjudication

---

**`AnnualCoverageAccumulator`** *(entity, keyed by member + year)*
Tracks how much of each coverage limit a member has consumed in a calendar year. Must be updated atomically when a claim is approved.
- `memberId`, `coverageYear`, `consumed: Map<ServiceCategory, Money>`

Invariant: `consumed[category]` never exceeds `CoverageLimit.annualMax` for that category on the member's policy.

---

**`Dispute`** *(aggregate root)*
Raised by a member against a closed claim decision.
- `disputeId`, `claimId`, `memberId`, `filedAt`
- `reason: DisputeReason`
- `status: DisputeStatus` *(see state machine)*
- `resolution: DisputeResolution | null`

Invariants:
- May only be filed against a `CLOSED` claim
- `resolution` may only be set when status is `UNDER_REVIEW`

---

### Value Objects

| Value Object | Fields | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Money` | `amount: Decimal`, `currency: ISO4217` | Immutable; arithmetic produces new instances |
| `ServiceDate` | `date: LocalDate` | Must be in the past |
| `ServiceCategory` | `code: string`, `description: string` | E.g. `PREVENTIVE`, `SPECIALIST`, `EMERGENCY` |
| `CoverageLimit` | `annualMax: Money`, `copay: Money`, `coinsuranceRate: Percentage` | Belongs to `Policy`, not `Claim` |
| `AdjudicationResult` | `approvedTotal: Money`, `lineItemDecisions: Map<LineItemId, Money>`, `processedAt: Instant` | Immutable snapshot of the adjudicator's decision |
| `DisputeReason` | `code: enum`, `freeText: string` | Codes: `INCORRECT_DENIAL`, `WRONG_AMOUNT`, `POLICY_MISAPPLIED` |
| `DisputeResolution` | `outcome: enum`, `revisedAmount: Money | null`, `resolvedAt: Instant` | Outcomes: `UPHELD`, `OVERTURNED`, `PARTIAL` |

---

### State Machines

**`ClaimStatus`**

```
SUBMITTED ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ UNDER_REVIEW ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ APPROVED
                    Ōöé
                    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ PARTIALLY_APPROVED
                    Ōöé
                    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ DENIED
                                    Ōöé
                              (dispute filed)
                                    Ōöé
                                    Ō¢╝
                                 CLOSED   ŌŚäŌöĆŌöĆ (all paths terminate here)
```

Transitions:
- `SUBMITTED ŌåÆ UNDER_REVIEW`: triggered when an adjudicator picks up the claim
- `UNDER_REVIEW ŌåÆ APPROVED | PARTIALLY_APPROVED | DENIED`: triggered by `AdjudicationResult` being recorded
- Any terminal status ŌåÆ `CLOSED`: after a grace period or explicit close action
- No transitions out of `CLOSED`

---

**`DisputeStatus`**

```
FILED ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ UNDER_REVIEW ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ RESOLVED
                Ōöé
                ŌööŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ WITHDRAWN
```

Transitions:
- `FILED ŌåÆ UNDER_REVIEW`: reviewer picks up the dispute
- `UNDER_REVIEW ŌåÆ RESOLVED`: `DisputeResolution` recorded; may re-open the underlying `Claim`
- `UNDER_REVIEW ŌåÆ WITHDRAWN`: member withdraws before resolution

---

### Key Invariants (summary)

| # | Invariant |
|---|---|
| I-1 | A `Claim` must have Ōēź 1 `LineItem` |
| I-2 | `LineItem.serviceDate` must fall within `Policy.effectiveDate ŌĆ” expirationDate` |
| I-3 | `LineItem.approvedAmount` Ōēż `LineItem.billedAmount` |
| I-4 | Sum of `approvedAmount` across line items = `AdjudicationResult.approvedTotal` |
| I-5 | Applying an `AdjudicationResult` must check `AnnualCoverageAccumulator` first; approval may be capped |
| I-6 | `AnnualCoverageAccumulator.consumed[category]` never exceeds `CoverageLimit.annualMax` |
| I-7 | A `Dispute` may only reference a `Claim` in `CLOSED` status |
| I-8 | A `CLOSED` `Claim` is immutable unless a `Dispute` is `RESOLVED` with outcome `OVERTURNED` or `PARTIAL` |
| I-9 | `Money` arithmetic never produces negative values |
| I-10 | `coinsuranceRate` is always in `[0.0, 1.0]` |

---

### Aggregate Boundaries (summary)

```
Claim (root)
  ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ LineItem[]

Dispute (root)
  ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ references ClaimId (no direct containment)

AnnualCoverageAccumulator (root)
  ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ keyed by (MemberId, Year)

Policy / Member  ŌåÉ read-only reference data for adjudication
```

The `Dispute` does not contain the `Claim` ŌĆö it holds a `ClaimId` reference. Resolving a dispute emits a domain event (`DisputeResolved`) that the `Claim` aggregate handles to re-open itself if warranted. This keeps the aggregates independently consistent.
